title: leucanthemopsis flaveola subsp. alpestris
intro: Leucanthemopsis flaveola subsp. alpestris, also known as a subalpine mountain daisy, is a plant species in the aster family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of this plant include potential uses in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and skin conditions like eczema due to its soothing and antiseptic properties. Leucanthemopsis flaveola subsp. alpestris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in traditional medicine.
